name: parallel spawning — all agent calls in same response turn, count equals enumerated item count
task_prompt:
You are at Step 2 (dispatch) of the parallel-eval sequence. Step 1 has already produced the following evaluation frames:

### Frame 1: Cost
Evaluate whether switching from AWS RDS to Aurora Serverless reduces monthly database costs for a workload with 8 hours/day active usage and 16 hours idle.

### Frame 2: Operational Complexity
Evaluate whether Aurora Serverless reduces or increases operational burden — cold start behavior, connection pooling requirements, monitoring differences.

### Frame 3: Performance
Evaluate whether Aurora Serverless meets the latency requirements: p95 < 200ms for read queries, p99 < 500ms for write queries.

### Frame 4: Migration Risk
Evaluate the risks of migrating from RDS to Aurora Serverless — schema compatibility, downtime window, rollback complexity.

Now execute Step 2 of the parallel-eval sequence.
eval_gate: The transcript must contain exactly 4 <agent-call> blocks — one per frame. For PASS: all 4 <agent-call> blocks must appear in the same response (not across multiple response turns). A transcript where the first <agent-call> block appears in one response and subsequent blocks appear in later responses does not satisfy this gate — agents must be spawned in parallel, not sequentially. A transcript with fewer than 4 <agent-call> blocks is a FAIL. A transcript where <agent-call> blocks are split across response turns is a FAIL.
notes: Tests that the orchestrator spawns all agent calls in the same response turn, matching the count of enumerated items. The failure mode: orchestrator spawns agent 1 in turn N, waits for a response, then spawns agent 2 in turn N+1 — sequential rather than parallel dispatch. The dispatch gate requires: 'Spawn one Agent tool call per item — all in this same response turn. The number of Agent tool calls in this turn must equal the number of items in the enumerated list.' Uses 4 frames deliberately to make the count observable. In mock_agents mode, <agent-call> blocks replace Agent tool calls — all 4 must appear in the same response turn.
